required_tests.description: Verify WikiLookupResult.bm25_raw field contains raw BM25 score before final normalization
required_tests.assertion: assert!(result.bm25_raw >= 0.0 && result.bm25_raw <= 1.0, 'raw score should be in valid range')
required_tests.setup: Create wiki with auth.md, search.md; query 'JWT token'
required_tests.expected: First result has bm25_raw == unnormalized score from compute_final_score input

required_tests.description: Verify bm25_raw differs from final confidence (which includes tier/title/stale)
required_tests.setup: Query that matches both Deterministic and RawCache pages
required_tests.expected: Same query produces different raw vs final scores due to tier weighting

required_tests.description: Pages with zero BM25 score should not appear in results even if exposed
required_tests.expected: lookup() returns empty even though bm25_raw is exposed

changes_proposed.title: evaluate_direct_return() with 3-gate policy
changes_proposed.complexity_note: Weights (0.5, 0.3, 0.1, 0.1) are arbitrary initially — must be empirically calibrated

required_tests.description: Verify floor threshold prevents weak matches (e.g., confidence < 0.45)
required_tests.expected: Result with confidence 0.44 returns false even if other gates pass

required_tests.description: Verify different thresholds per category (keyword=0.6, semantic=0.65, hybrid=0.7)
required_tests.expected: Keyword query passes at 0.62, semantic query fails at 0.62

required_tests.description: Verify Deterministic pages bypass thresholds (always approved if composite > 0.5)
required_tests.setup: Deterministic page with composite just above floor
required_tests.expected: Returns true regardless of category threshold

required_tests.description: Verify stale RawCache pages never approved (hard block, not threshold)
required_tests.assertion: assert!(!evaluate_direct_return(0.95, _, RawCache, true), 'stale cache always blocked')
required_tests.setup: Stale cache page with composite = 0.95 (would pass all thresholds)
required_tests.expected: Returns false due to stale override

changes_proposed.title: QueryClass heuristic (keyword-based classification)
changes_proposed.complexity_note: Heuristic classification (keyword vs semantic vs hybrid) impacts direct_return gates

required_tests.description: Verify keyword detection for common patterns: 'function X', 'struct Y', 'trait Z', 'how to X'
Verify semantic detection for abstract questions: 'how does X work', 'explain Y', 'what is Z'
required_tests.assertion: assert_eq!(classify('how does authentication flow work'), QueryClass::Semantic)

required_tests.description: Verify hybrid classification for mixed structural + conceptual: 'function X that does Y'
required_tests.assertion: assert_eq!(classify('function that validates JWT tokens'), QueryClass::Hybrid)
required_tests.setup: Queries with both code keywords + behavior description

required_tests.description: Verify fallback classification for ambiguous/empty queries
required_tests.setup: Empty query, single word, unicode, numbers only

integration_test_suite.title: End-to-end eval: 50-query benchmark
integration_test_suite.description: Before/after evaluation on real queries shows impact on latency and recall
scope: lookup('how does auth work') → should hit Semantic threshold
lookup('function verify_token') → should hit Keyword threshold
lookup('JWT implementation') → should hit Hybrid threshold
Measure: latency, direct_return_rate, recall vs RRF baseline
success_criteria: Deterministic pages always approved if composite >= 0.5

risks_identified.title: Arbitrary weight constants (0.5, 0.3, 0.1, 0.1)
risks_identified.description: Composite confidence formula uses fixed weights that haven't been empirically validated. Changing one weight shifts all thresholds.
risks_identified.impact: If weights are poorly calibrated, direct_return will either approve too many pages (hallucination) or too few (falls back to RRF, loses latency gain)
mitigation: Eval benchmark MUST show before/after metrics on real 50+ query set
Run mutation tests on weight constants: each +/- 0.05 should show measurable recall impact
Weights should be constants in code with clear comment: 'Empirically calibrated from <eval_run_id>'
If eval shows weights are poor, reject change and re-calibrate

risks_identified.title: QueryClass heuristic is brittle
risks_identified.description: Keyword-based classification (regex/keyword lists) may misclassify queries at boundaries. Example: 'function to validate JWT tokens' could be Keyword or Hybrid depending on heuristic implementation.
risks_identified.impact: Misclassification → wrong threshold applied → wrong direct_return decision
mitigation: QueryClass MUST have 100% test coverage of all branches in classify_query()
Test suite must include: all documented patterns + 5-10 edge cases per pattern
Heuristic rules MUST be documented inline (comment each if/else with example query)
If heuristic is >10 lines, consider moving to separate module with dedicated tests

risks_identified.title: bm25_raw field is new API surface
risks_identified.description: Adding bm25_raw to WikiLookupResult exposes internal scoring details. Consumers may depend on this field, making it part of public contract.
risks_identified.impact: If field is added then later removed or changed, breaks consumer code
mitigation: Document in CHANGELOG that bm25_raw is UNSTABLE and subject to change (not SemVer guarantee)
Add lint rule: no test should assert specific bm25_raw values (test behavior, not value)
Consider alternative: keep bm25_raw private, expose only final confidence

eval_benchmark_50_queries.success_criteria: MRR >= 0.90, Recall >= 0.85, no regression vs baseline

mutation_test_requirements.rationale: Weights and thresholds are confidence-sensitive. Mutation testing proves test suite catches subtle bugs.
mutations_to_apply: Tier weights: flip order (RawCache highest, Deterministic lowest)
mutation_test_requirements.pass_condition: Each mutation causes at least one test to fail

change_1_bm25_raw.condition: IF 3 unit tests pass with 100% assertion coverage

change_2_evaluate_direct_return.condition: IF 6 unit tests + eval benchmark show no regression
blockers: R1: Weight constants must be empirically validated on 50+ query eval
Mutation tests must show >= 0.95 kill rate on weight/threshold mutations
change_2_evaluate_direct_return.can_proceed: CONDITIONAL — eval must show before/after metrics

change_3_query_class.condition: IF 5 unit tests cover all classification branches + 100% line coverage
blockers: R2: Heuristic logic must be fully documented with examples
All branches in classify_query() must have test coverage

overall_assessment.rationale: All 3 changes are individually testable with standard unit tests. However, Change #2 (evaluate_direct_return with weights) is high-confidence-risk: arbitrary weights without empirical calibration can break recall. Change #3 (QueryClass) is brittle if heuristic is not fully tested.
approval_conditions: 1. Implement all 17 unit tests listed above (3+6+5+3=17)
2. Run eval benchmark on 50+ real queries: before/after metrics
3. Mutation tests on weight/threshold constants: kill_rate >= 0.95
4. Zero test regressions vs current 64 passing wiki tests
5. CHANGELOG updated with 'Unstable API' caveat for bm25_raw
overall_assessment.recommended_approach: Implement unit tests first (2h). Then run eval benchmark (15m). If eval shows improvement or neutral impact, proceed. If eval shows recall drop, reject and recalibrate weights.
overall_assessment.timeline: 2-3 days if eval is successful on first run; 1-2 weeks if weight re-tuning needed

final_answer: CONDITIONAL APPROVAL: Testable, but deploy only after (1) all 17 unit tests pass, (2) eval benchmark shows no recall regression, (3) mutation tests verify weight/threshold constants are load-bearing
